meta.title: Farever loot tables (CastleDB)
meta.notes: Each loot entry typically includes proba (probability). Entries may specify item (CastleDB item id), lootTable (nested table), optional minLvl/maxLvl, itemMin/itemMax, conds, flags. Exact roll semantics are defined by game code. For entries with item, name and flavorDesc are resolved from the item sheet (following texts.inherit when present). In flavorDesc, tokens like [ZoneId] or [JobId] are replaced with display names from zone, job, attribute, skill, gameTerm, and unitType rows (whole [id] is substituted, brackets removed).

entries.name: Simple Pearl
entries.flavorDesc: Usually found on creatures in aquatic areas of Skover Island.

entries.name: Simple Pearl
entries.flavorDesc: Usually found on creatures in aquatic areas of Skover Island.

entries.name: Sticky Fin
entries.flavorDesc: A sticky fin from Nepsids, vile creatures roaming coastal areas in Skover Island and Valley of Eternal Autumn.

entries.name: Small Mackerel
entries.flavorDesc: Caught in Skover Island's waters and can be eaten raw or prepared to cook meals.

entries.name: Pike
entries.flavorDesc: Caught in Valley of Eternal Autumn's waters and can be eaten raw or prepared to cook meals.

entries.name: Sharp Scale
entries.flavorDesc: A sharp fin from Nepsids, vile creatures roaming coastal areas in Valley of Eternal Autumn.

entries.name: Meropsian Wingfish
entries.flavorDesc: Wingfish are truly strange creatures: nature has endowed them with wings for swimming and fins for flying, which, contrary to what one might think, makes them very poor at both.

entries.name: Simple Pearl
entries.flavorDesc: Usually found on creatures in aquatic areas of Skover Island.

entries.name: Simple Pearl
entries.flavorDesc: Usually found on creatures in aquatic areas of Skover Island.

entries.name: Fleshy Claw
entries.flavorDesc: Collected from crabs in Skover Island and can be eaten raw or prepared to cook meals.

entries.name: Crab Egg
entries.flavorDesc: Collected from crabs in Valley of Eternal Autumn and can be eaten raw or prepared to cook meals.

entries.name: Eyestalk
entries.flavorDesc: An eyestalk found on crabs, roaming the aquatic areas in Skover Island.

entries.name: Antelimbian Crab
entries.flavorDesc: Legend has it that the first crab used as a mount was won by a fisherman during a charades match against a Nepsid. The word to guess was "crab". The match lasted two days.

entries.name: Kobold Swiss
entries.flavorDesc: Collected from humanoid creatures in Skover Island and can be eaten raw or prepared to cook meals.

entries.name: Ramburg Bleu
entries.flavorDesc: Collected from humanoid creatures in Skover Island and can be eaten raw or prepared to cook meals.

entries.name: Stale Bread
entries.flavorDesc: Collected from humanoids and can be eaten raw or prepared to cook meals.

entries.name: Small Fang
entries.flavorDesc: Usually found on creatures with fangs like wolves, skunks or even Kobolds in Skover Island and Valley of Eternal Autumn.

entries.name: Piece of Perforated Metal
entries.flavorDesc: Kobolds wear some of these in Valley of Eternal Autumn.

entries.name: Enripian Bat
entries.flavorDesc: The bats of Siagarta have developed the unfortunate habit of drinking their master's blood while they sleep.

entries.name: Honey
entries.flavorDesc: Collected from bees in Skover Island and can be eaten raw or prepared to cook meals.

entries.name: Diaphanous Wings
entries.flavorDesc: Usually found on flying creatures, mainly bees, in Skover Island.

entries.name: Glossy Chitin
entries.flavorDesc: Usually found on bees from Valley of Eternal Autumn.

entries.name: Zerzurean Leggybug
entries.flavorDesc: Originally bred by farmers to rid their crops of midges, leggybugs eventually grew big enough to be employed as draught animals, and later as recreational vehicles.

entries.name: Diaphanous Wings
entries.flavorDesc: Usually found on flying creatures, mainly bees, in Skover Island.

entries.name: Zerzurean Leggybug

entries.name: Ramgold
entries.flavorDesc: Obtained by defeating Crimson Rams, this strange material seems infused with power from another world. Can be used by several jobs.

entries.name: Stale Bread
entries.flavorDesc: Collected from humanoids and can be eaten raw or prepared to cook meals.

entries.name: Pumpkin
entries.flavorDesc: Collected from humanoids and can be eaten raw or prepared to cook meals.

entries.name: Ramburg Bleu
entries.flavorDesc: Collected from humanoid creatures in Skover Island and can be eaten raw or prepared to cook meals.

entries.name: Fountrailian Hound
entries.flavorDesc: In the Crimson city of Ramburg, hounds are prized as a status symbol. A deep red robe indicates that the hound's master is a bloodthirsty monster, even by the Crimsons' standards.

entries.name: Sweet Root
entries.flavorDesc: Collected from living plants and can be eaten raw or prepared to cook meals.

entries.name: Wheat
entries.flavorDesc: Collected from humanoids and can be eaten raw or prepared to cook meals.

entries.name: Eksodean Seedbird
entries.flavorDesc: When Seedbirds are hungry, it’s said that they nibble on the tips of their roots and eventually fall to the ground after eating their wings. There, they grow back the following season.

entries.name: Fresh Blood
entries.flavorDesc: A small vial of blood that can be collected from any living creature.

entries.name: Wolf Meat
entries.flavorDesc: Collected from wolves in Skover Island and can be eaten raw or prepared to cook meals.

entries.name: Small Fang
entries.flavorDesc: Usually found on creatures with fangs like wolves, skunks or even Kobolds in Skover Island and Valley of Eternal Autumn.

entries.name: Nescentine Wolf
entries.flavorDesc: Wolves were trained to sniff out treasures hidden under the debris. Sadly, this one lost the sense of smell after a violent encounter with a skunk.

entries.name: Skunk Meat
entries.flavorDesc: Collected from skunks  in Skover Island and can be eaten raw or prepared to cook meals.

entries.name: Fresh Blood
entries.flavorDesc: A small vial of blood that can be collected from any living creature.

entries.name: Small Fang
entries.flavorDesc: Usually found on creatures with fangs like wolves, skunks or even Kobolds in Skover Island and Valley of Eternal Autumn.

entries.name: Primevallean Skunk
entries.flavorDesc: Skunks were formerly seen as a nuisance. That was before tamers found out the smell problem could be fixed by feeding them only flowers.

entries.name: Fresh Blood
entries.flavorDesc: A small vial of blood that can be collected from any living creature.

entries.name: Coyote Meat
entries.flavorDesc: Collected from coyotes and can be eaten raw or prepared to cook meals.

entries.name: Fresh Blood
entries.flavorDesc: A small vial of blood that can be collected from any living creature.

entries.name: Chipped Tusk
entries.flavorDesc: Usually found on creatures with defenses such as wild boars in Skover Island.

entries.name: Fat
entries.flavorDesc: Collected from boars in Valley of Eternal Autumn and can be eaten raw or prepared to cook meals.

entries.name: Boar Meat
entries.flavorDesc: Collected from boars in Skover Island and can be eaten raw or prepared to cook meals.

entries.name: Meridional Hog
entries.flavorDesc: The most efficient way to travel is not to run fast, but to avoid running into foes. In that regard, the intimidating stare of the hog is more useful than the buttontail's sprint.

entries.name: Ephemeral Heart
entries.flavorDesc: Usually found on spirits roaming ruins in Valley of Eternal Autumn.

entries.name: Spark Shard
entries.flavorDesc: Earned from completing activities or killing bosses. Can be used to upgrade weapons at a village.

entries.name: Spark Shard
entries.flavorDesc: Earned from completing activities or killing bosses. Can be used to upgrade weapons at a village.

entries.name: Egheretrian Wingfish
entries.flavorDesc: Wingfish are truly strange creatures: nature has endowed them with wings for swimming and fins for flying, which, contrary to what one might think, makes them very poor at both.

entries.name: Semeruian Crab
entries.flavorDesc: Legend has it that the first crab used as a mount was won by a fisherman during a charades match against a Nepsid. The word to guess was "crab". The match lasted two days.

entries.name: Eksodean Leggybug
entries.flavorDesc: Originally bred by farmers to rid their crops of midges, leggybugs eventually grew big enough to be employed as draught animals, and later as recreational vehicles.

entries.name: Semeruian Dragoon
entries.flavorDesc: High above Skover’s floating fields, the local creatures had to adjust fast to life in the sky. That’s how the glider-drakes came to be small, friendly dragon pets with soft wings and an easy glide. They flit between the hovering cliffs like playful paper kites, happy to follow anyone who offers a warm hand and a little adventure.

entries.name: Meropsian Moth
entries.flavorDesc: Moths are fascinating creatures capable of releasing powders with various effects: medicinal, hallucinogenic, soporific, poisonous... You should always avoid touching a moth with your fingers.

entries.name: Egheretrian Seedbird
entries.flavorDesc: When Seedbirds are hungry, it’s said that they nibble on the tips of their roots and eventually fall to the ground after eating their wings. There, they grow back the following season.

entries.name: Ghost Clams of the Low Tide
entries.flavorDesc: Before Lord Rulean stole this artifact from the Nepsids, they thought it was responsible for the tides. Luckily, the theft proved that it was in fact useless.

entries.name: Iron Fins of the Leviathan
entries.flavorDesc: Having fins of iron is not necessarily an advantage. In fact, it made the Leviathan slow enough for the fishermen to harvest its precious fins.

entries.name: Antelimbian Wingfish
entries.flavorDesc: Wingfish are truly strange creatures: nature has endowed them with wings for swimming and fins for flying, which, contrary to what one might think, makes them very poor at both.

entries.name: Flame of Argol
entries.flavorDesc: Burning since before the war of the Four Hundred, this candle is widely regarded as the living soul of the legendary Argol Konda.

entries.name: Cheese Moon
entries.flavorDesc: Forged in the depths of Irukal, this axe was designed by a people who had never seen a moon in their life, and it shows.

entries.name: Navelian Bat
entries.flavorDesc: The bats of Siagarta have developed the unfortunate habit of drinking their master's blood while they sleep.

entries.name: Crabgantua's Kneecap
entries.flavorDesc: A creature with three pairs of legs should have more than one kneecap. Crabgantua only had one, but its size and hardness is admirable.

entries.name: Clawdius
entries.flavorDesc: At the end of a warrior's arms, these claws look like the gluttonous mouths of two giant snakes.

entries.name: Meropsian Crab
entries.flavorDesc: Legend has it that the first crab used as a mount was won by a fisherman during a charades match against a Nepsid. The word to guess was "crab". The match lasted two days.

entries.name: Twin Fangs of Ratsar
entries.flavorDesc: Like most rodents, Ratsar doesn't have fangs, so he had to steal these. Someday, another toothless creature will probably take them from him too.

entries.name: Judgement
entries.flavorDesc: The institutions of justice are rather uncomplicated in Siagarta. This single axe, for instance, cumulates legislative, judiciary and executive power.

entries.name: Krisomalese Bat
entries.flavorDesc: The bats of Siagarta have developed the unfortunate habit of drinking their master's blood while they sleep.

entries.name: Thornlace
entries.flavorDesc: A masterpiece of Apix craft, Thornlace is also an expression of the clan's philosophy: beauty is a warning, always laced with danger.

entries.name: Lady Bee’s Ceremonial Stinger
entries.flavorDesc: In the past, Apix princesses ennobled royal guards using their own stingers, but a series of incidents convinced them to use that spear instead.

entries.name: Semeruian Moth
entries.flavorDesc: Moths are fascinating creatures capable of releasing powders with various effects: medicinal, hallucinogenic, soporific, poisonous... You should always avoid touching a moth with your fingers.

entries.name: Ipheion, Star Blossom
entries.flavorDesc: Starflowers are pretty common, but few of them are shooting stars. And even fewer can cut an armor in half.

entries.name: Beefury, Blessed Blade of the Farseeker
entries.flavorDesc: There is a metaphor at play here: you can see through the blade, but the blade can cut through you. This is the kind of poetry the Apix clan enjoys.

entries.name: Nescentine Leggybug
entries.flavorDesc: Originally bred by farmers to rid their crops of midges, leggybugs eventually grew big enough to be employed as draught animals, and later as recreational vehicles.

entries.name: Gorgon Ratsay’s Toothpick
entries.flavorDesc: Although the legendary Ratsay used his spear to hunt, cook and eat, this tool was mostly remembered as the first toothpick in Kobold history.

entries.name: Magma Mia
entries.flavorDesc: According to legend, this is an overcooked slice of pizza from the Primeval Age. Some scholars pretend it is just cheese. Either way, no one ever managed to eat it.

entries.name: Ebral Dragoon
entries.flavorDesc: High above Skover’s floating fields, the local creatures had to adjust fast to life in the sky. That’s how the glider-drakes came to be small, friendly dragon pets with soft wings and an easy glide. They flit between the hovering cliffs like playful paper kites, happy to follow anyone who offers a warm hand and a little adventure.

entries.name: Book of Mi'Mizan
entries.flavorDesc: Mizan is known for travelling around Merops to learn Naya magic from the Nepsids. No one knows what he did there, but they're no longer taking apprentices.

entries.name: Horns of the Wind
entries.flavorDesc: This weapon is a testimony to the Rams' lack of imagination. If something can hurt them, they always picture it wearing a pair of horns.

entries.name: Acidic Wingfish
entries.flavorDesc: Wingfish are truly strange creatures: nature has endowed them with wings for swimming and fins for flying, which, contrary to what one might think, makes them very poor at both.

entries.name: Twin Pillars of Justice
entries.flavorDesc: These weapons have little to do with justice and they certainly aren't pillars, but the Crimson Rams have a taste for metaphors.

entries.name: Amon Ram, the Creator
entries.flavorDesc: Amon, ram-headed god of creation, appeared to Lady Skover in a vision. No one really believed her story at the time, but it did make the Rams appear more legitimate.

entries.name: Rinuhrian Skunk
entries.flavorDesc: Skunks were formerly seen as a nuisance. That was before tamers found out the smell problem could be fixed by feeding them only flowers.

entries.name: Pocket Hive
entries.flavorDesc: It's unclear why these specific bees never evolved like their humanoid sisters, but owning one of these tiny hives has become a status symbol for Apix royalty.

entries.name: Wingsabers
entries.flavorDesc: The sudden evolution of the bees' appearance triggered a sort of identity crisis, after which they started making all their stuff look like wings.

entries.name: Ponogian Leggybug
entries.flavorDesc: Originally bred by farmers to rid their crops of midges, leggybugs eventually grew big enough to be employed as draught animals, and later as recreational vehicles.

entries.name: Fresh Blood
entries.flavorDesc: A small vial of blood that can be collected from any living creature.

entries.name: Mote of Naya
entries.flavorDesc: A small water mote found on aquatic creatures in Skover Island.

entries.name: Mote of Pyrh
entries.flavorDesc: A small fire mote found on fire creatures in Skover Island.

entries.name: Mote of Mely
entries.flavorDesc: A small natural mote found on living creatures in Skover Island.

entries.name: Mote of Kyre
entries.flavorDesc: A small earth mote found on rocky creatures in Skover Island.

entries.name: Mote of Aoyl
entries.flavorDesc: A small air mote found on wind creatures in Skover Island.

entries.name: Mote of Naya
entries.flavorDesc: A small water mote found on aquatic creatures in Skover Island.

entries.name: Mote of Pyrh
entries.flavorDesc: A small fire mote found on fire creatures in Skover Island.

entries.flavorDesc: A small natural mote found on living creatures in Skover Island.

entries.name: Mote of Kyre
entries.flavorDesc: A small earth mote found on rocky creatures in Skover Island.

entries.name: Mote of Aoyl
entries.flavorDesc: A small air mote found on wind creatures in Skover Island.

entries.name: Linen Cloth
entries.flavorDesc: Usually found on humanoid creatures in Skover Island.

entries.name: Amber
entries.flavorDesc: Sometimes gathered from deposits in Skover Island or acquired by crushing ores, this raw crystal can be cut into a gemstone.

entries.name: Beryl
entries.flavorDesc: Sometimes gathered from deposits in Skover Island or acquired by crushing ores, this raw crystal can be cut into a gemstone.

entries.name: Ruby
entries.flavorDesc: Sometimes gathered from deposits in Valley of Eternal Autumn or acquired by crushing ores, this raw crystal can be cut into a gemstone.

entries.name: Agate
entries.flavorDesc: Sometimes gathered from deposits in Valley of Eternal Autumn or acquired by crushing ores, this raw crystal can be cut into a gemstone.

entries.name: Soft Fur
entries.flavorDesc: A rare unspoiled fur found on wild animals in Valley of Eternal Autumn.

entries.name: Soft Fur
entries.flavorDesc: A rare unspoiled fur found on wild animals in Valley of Eternal Autumn.

entries.name: Light Leather Strap
entries.flavorDesc: Usually found on wild animals in Skover Island.

entries.name: Copper Ore
entries.flavorDesc: Gathered from deposits in Skover Island, this can be smelted into ingots or crushed to find precious stones.

entries.name: Cracked Stone
entries.flavorDesc: Usually found on creatures or from deposits in rocky areas of Skover Island.

entries.name: Tin Ore
entries.flavorDesc: Gathered from deposits in Skover Island, this can be smelted into ingots or crushed to find precious stones.

entries.name: Coal
entries.flavorDesc: Gathered in volcanic areas in Valley of Eternal Autumn, this can be added to iron to craft steel.

entries.name: Tungstene Ore
entries.flavorDesc: A pretty rare ore gathered from deposits in Skover Island. This can be smelted into ingots or crushed to find precious stones.

entries.name: Madrigold Petal
entries.flavorDesc: Gathered from plants in Skover Island, this can be milled into powders or used to season meals.

entries.name: Lavendula Petal
entries.flavorDesc: Gathered from plants in Skover Island, this can be milled into powders or used to season meals.

entries.name: Ancient Thyme Petal
entries.flavorDesc: Gathered from plants in Skover Island, this can be milled into powders or used to season meals.

entries.name: Zealotus Petal
entries.flavorDesc: A pretty rare petal gathered from plants in Skover Island. This can be milled into powders or used to season meals.

entries.name: Spark Dust
entries.flavorDesc: Earned from various creatures, completing activities or opening crates. Can be used to ugprade weapons at a village.

entries.flavorDesc: Having fins of iron is not necessarily an advantage. In fact, it made the Leviathan slow enough for the fishermen to harvest its precious fins.

entries.name: Radiance
entries.flavorDesc: Like a sunbeam hitting the dewy blossoms of the morning, this long wooden beam descends vigorously upon the skulls and noses of the unworthy.

entries.name: Credence
entries.flavorDesc: A bow is an object of faith and each arrow is a mystery. Will it hit its target? Will its target hit back? Is there still time for a second shot?
